display.purpose: Hero name only. The single largest element on any page.

headline.purpose: Section entry headlines and contact CTA.

label.purpose: Section numbers, eyebrows, nav links, role titles. Always uppercase.

shadows.name: panel-lift
shadows.purpose: Case study register only. Floating animation panels.

shadows.name: panel-border
shadows.purpose: Case study register only. Terminal panel edge on dark backgrounds.

shadows.name: modern-panel
shadows.purpose: Case study register only. Web-modernized panels, lighter lift.

shadows.name: phosphor-glow
shadows.purpose: Case study register only. CRT terminal phosphor text radiation.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Every transition in the system. One curve, no exceptions.

breakpoints.name: mobile
breakpoints.purpose: Nav collapses, section padding reduces, about-grid goes single column.

components.name: Skill Tag
components.description: Pill-shaped label for skills. Border only, no fill. Accent on hover.
components.css: .ds-skill-tag { display: inline-block; font-family: 'Epilogue', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 400; color: #57534E; padding: 8px 16px 6px; border: 1px solid #E4E2DC; border-radius: 100px; line-height: 1; transition: border-color 300ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), color 300ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); cursor: default; } .ds-skill-tag:hover { color: var(--accent, #D4D0CA); border-color: var(--accent, #D4D0CA); }

components.name: Hero Link
components.description: Pill link in the hero section. Same shape as skill tag, slightly larger padding.
components.css: .ds-hero-link { display: inline-block; font-family: 'Epilogue', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.04em; color: #57534E; padding: 13px 24px 11px; border: 1px solid #E4E2DC; border-radius: 100px; line-height: 1; text-decoration: none; transition: border-color 400ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), color 400ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-hero-link:hover { border-color: #1C1917; color: #1C1917; }

components.name: Nav Link
components.description: Navigation link. Uppercase micro-label, muted at rest, accent on hover/active.
components.css: .ds-nav-link { font-family: 'Epilogue', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.6875rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #736D67; text-decoration: none; transition: color 400ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-nav-link:hover, .ds-nav-link.is-active { color: var(--accent, #D4D0CA); }

components.name: Theme Toggle
components.description: Circle icon button. Border only, accent on hover. Spins on click.
components.css: .ds-theme-toggle { width: 34px; height: 34px; border-radius: 50%; border: 1px solid #E4E2DC; background: transparent; color: #736D67; cursor: pointer; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-size: 0.875rem; transition: border-color 500ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), color 500ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-theme-toggle:hover { border-color: var(--accent, #D4D0CA); color: var(--accent, #D4D0CA); } @keyframes ds-toggle-spin { 0% { transform: scale(1) rotate(0deg); } 45% { transform: scale(0.4) rotate(180deg); } 100% { transform: scale(1) rotate(360deg); } } .ds-toggle-icon.is-spinning { animation: ds-toggle-spin 0.5s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); }

components.name: Timeline Item
components.description: Experience entry. Flat list item separated by border-top. Company name slides on hover.
components.html:
01
Taylor & Johnson2018–2021
Multimedia Engineer

Modernización de interfaces AS-400 con Presto.
components.css: .ds-timeline-item { padding: 36px 0; border-top: 1px solid transparent; transition: border-color 400ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); cursor: default; } .ds-timeline-item + .ds-timeline-item { border-top-color: #E4E2DC; } .ds-timeline-num { display: block; font-size: 0.6875rem; font-weight: 400; color: var(--accent, #D4D0CA); letter-spacing: 0.04em; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; margin-bottom: 12px; transition: color 600ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-timeline-header { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: baseline; margin-bottom: 10px; } .ds-timeline-company { font-size: 1.375rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: -0.03em; color: #1C1917; transition: color 400ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), transform 350ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-timeline-period { font-size: 0.6875rem; color: #736D67; letter-spacing: 0.06em; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-timeline-role { font-size: 0.6875rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #736D67; margin-bottom: 10px; } .ds-timeline-desc { font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.75; color: #57534E; max-width: 65ch; } .ds-timeline-item:hover .ds-timeline-company { color: var(--accent, #D4D0CA); transform: translateX(6px); }

components.name: Case Study Entry Link
components.description: Text link at the bottom of a timeline item that leads to a case study route. Silent at rest, accent on hover.
components.css: .ds-case-link { display: inline-block; font-family: 'Epilogue', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #736D67; text-decoration: none; transition: color 400ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); margin-top: 16px; } .ds-case-link:hover { color: var(--accent, #2563EB); }

narrative.overview:
This system gives nothing away unprompted. Color is earned by scroll. Hierarchy is earned by attention. The ambient bloom exists only where something is active. The design operates on a single principle: everything is conditional on context, and that conditionality is the craft.

The palette begins in monochrome and unlocks section by section. This is not a scroll animation gimmick. It is the argument: a Design Engineer who can build a system where the interface itself demonstrates taste without announcing it.

Two visual registers coexist. The CV register is flat, editorial, typographically driven. The case study register is cinematic — deep shadows, phosphor glow, glass panels — because the work being shown demands immersion.
keyCharacteristics: Monochromatic at rest, chromatic in context
Typography carries hierarchy; components stay silent
Flat in the CV register, cinematic in case studies
Warm stone undertones throughout; never pure black or pure white

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Only one accent is active at a time. --accent on :root is the single source of truth. Components reference var(--accent), never a hardcoded section color. Rarity is the point.

rules.name: The Warmth Rule
rules.body: Every neutral leans warm. No cool grays, no pure blacks, no optical whites. The undertone is always toward stone and amber, never toward blue or green.

rules.name: The Atmospheric Exception
rules.body: The ambient bloom is the only permitted depth element in the CV register. It is not an elevation token. It is climate.

rules.name: The Single Family Rule
rules.body: Epilogue handles every weight. Never introduce a second display face. The hero name at weight 900 and a label at weight 600 are the same font — that coherence is visible.

dos: Do reference var(--accent) for all interactive color states. Never hardcode a section color in a component.
Do use cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) for every transition. One curve, everywhere.
Do enforce 65ch max line length on body text.
Do keep the CV register flat. Introduce shadows only in case study routes.
Do use prefers-reduced-motion to disable transform-based animations while preserving opacity and color transitions.
Do lean warm on every neutral. Tint toward stone and amber, never toward blue or green.
Do treat the ambient bloom as climate, not structure.
donts: Don't use gradient text. Prohibited. Use var(--text-strong) or var(--accent) as a solid color.
Don't use glassmorphism decoratively in the CV register. Glass belongs exclusively in case study immersive contexts.
Don't create hero-metric layouts outside of the About section stats.
Don't use identical card grids. If items look the same, question whether they need to be cards.
Don't use transition: all. Specify exact properties.
Don't introduce a second display typeface.
Don't add decorative side-stripe borders (border-left > 1px as accent).
Don't bring case study visual language into the CV register. The contrast between registers is the design.
